nytimes-2022.text: Jan. 6 HearingsThe House committee will hold its fourth hearing on the Capitol riot

LIVEJan. 6 Panel to Highlight Trump’s Pressure on State OfficialsThe panel plans to detail former President Trump’s personal involvement in a pressure campaign on state officials to subvert the will of the voters.The hearing at 1 p.m. Eastern will also underscore the suffering election workers endured because of Mr. Trump’s lies. Follow updates here.President Trump pushed Brad Raffensperger, Georgia’s secretary of state, to “find” him enough votes to put the state in his column. Audra Melton for The New York Times

BREAKINGHead of Texas Police Calls Response to Uvalde Shooting an ‘Abject Failure’The director of the Department of Public Safety said that police officers wasted time looking for a classroom door key that was “never needed.”
Supreme Court Rejects Maine’s Ban on Aid to Religious SchoolsThe decision was the latest in a series of rulings forbidding the exclusion of religious institutions from government programs.
Here are the major Supreme Court decisions in 2022 so far.
Gabor Degre/The Bangor Daily News, via Associated Press

LIVEDemand for Russian Oil Surges in Asia, Blunting Effect of SanctionsChina and India are snapping up Russian crude, dulling the West’s efforts to punish Moscow over its invasion of Ukraine. Here’s the latest.

In Ukraine, One Woman’s Shopping Trip Over a Destroyed BridgeShelling has mostly destroyed critical bridges in the battle for Sievierodonetsk and Lysychansk, but that didn’t stop one woman from crossing to buy some basics.
Svitlana Zhyvaga, 54, crossing one of the destroyed bridges still being used by civilians to go back and forth from Lysychansk to Sievierodonetsk. Tyler Hicks/The New York Times
As Russia makes slow progress, the arrival of new weapons systems will help Ukraine hold territory, U.S. officials say.
In a visit to Kyiv, Merrick Garland named a prosecutor known for investigating Nazis to help track Russian war criminals.
China’s Surveillance State Is Growing. These Documents Reveal How.
Here are four takeaways from a Times investigation into China’s expanding surveillance state.
时报调查揭示中国如何成为监控国家，这里是四大要点 (Read the takeaways in Chinese.)

LIVEEffort to Vaccinate Very Young U.S. Children Gets UnderwayProviding shots to children under 5 is a milestone in the immunization effort, but it is being greeted with mixed emotions. Follow pandemic news.
Broadway announced that it would drop its mask mandates in theaters starting July 1.
Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times

Microsoft Plans to Eliminate Face Analysis Tools in Push for ‘Responsible A.I.’The technology giant will stop offering automated tools that predict a person’s gender and age, and will restrict the use of its facial recognition tool.“We’re taking concrete steps to live up to our A.I. principles,” said Natasha Crampton, Microsoft’s chief responsible A.I. officer. Grant Hindsley for The New York Times

Hong Kong’s Floating Restaurant Sinks at Sea, Laden With MemoriesJumbo Floating Restaurant, which closed in 2020, capsized after being towed from the city, triggering nostalgia for a happier time in Hong Kong.Peter Parks/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

Retired at 4, Wasabi Still Carries Himself Like a ChampionLast year’s Westminster dog show winner was not in it for the money and he is very much a dog, even if it can be hard to tell from a distance.Andrew Mangum for The New York Times
